_instructions: This is ONE combined file describing an entire Knacktor problem. Fill every field, then bring it back to the repo and run: npm run import-problem <thisFile.json>
Keys starting with _ are notes for you and are ignored by the importer — you may delete them.

ADDING_PROBLEMS.md is the full authoring PROMPT — obey every rule in it and run its §10 self-validation before you output. Fill EVERY line: each approach needs a lineExplanations AND a syntaxExplanations AND a narration.byLine entry for every code line, and a phaseRules mapping for every executable line.

Only the 'array' and 'bar-container' primitives exist. The expression DSL allows + - * / %, comparisons, && || !, ternary, indexing, and only the functions min, max, len, abs. The cell index in cellStateRules is 'idx' (NOT the solution's i). A var's previous-step value is '<name>_prev'.

'solution' is the REAL Python source as a string with \n line breaks. Line numbers are load-bearing — lineExplanations / syntaxExplanations / mapping / narration all key off them. Keep one class Solution with the entrypoint method.
Presets: >=3, >=1 with isEdgeCase true, every preset has the correct expectedOutput, and together they execute every branch of every approach.
title: REPLACE problem title
statement: REPLACE with the FULL exact problem statement. Use \n for line breaks.

approaches.name: REPLACE approach name

lineExplanations.3: REPLACE — what this line does in the algorithm.

syntaxExplanations.2: def declares the method; self is the object, nums is the input.
syntaxExplanations.3: REPLACE — beginner syntax for this line.
